what_it_does: Records a raised decision signal that the operator chose the option 'Push the drawn style further first' for the global visual-fidelity decision and states that work gated on that decision may resume.
observations: The file is a noticeboard signal with id item-761be915 and address board://item-761be915
Type is 'signal', source is 'claude_code', state is 'raised', author_session is 'operator'
Created and updated timestamps are identical: 2026-06-22T01:11:54.033113+00:00
History contains a single transition from null to 'raised' by operator with note 'filed'
The body explicitly calls this a 'decision.decided signal' and notes that the resume itself is the lane's/operator's gated step
standouts: The chosen option is 'Push the drawn style further first' for visual-fidelity
It is attached to the decision URI decision://global/visual-fidelity
It frames resumption of gated work as the lane's/operator's gated step, not automatic
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, links, timestamps, and history
Uses URI schemes board:// and decision:// for addressing
Follows a signal/noticeboard pattern with a Markdown body explaining the signal meaning
concerns: channel and thread fields are empty strings
No rationale for why this option was chosen is captured
The resume step is mentioned but no specific lane or work item is identified
notes: This is a raised decision signal that records the operator's chosen visual-fidelity option and signals that gated downstream work may resume.
questions: What were the alternative options for visual-fidelity?
Which lane or work items are gated on decision://global/visual-fidelity?
What does 'drawn style' refer to in this context?
What criteria or rationale led to selecting this option?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file presents itself as a decision.decided signal and indeed records the chosen option and the unblocking condition.
intention_signals: To notify the system that the visual-fidelity decision is resolved with a specific option so that dependent work waiting on it can proceed.
novelty: The explicit framing that the resume itself is the lane's/operator's gated step, rather than treating the decision signal as automatically unblocking work.
contribution: A timestamped, attributable record of which visual-fidelity option was decided and the associated decision URI, giving downstream processes a concrete signal that the gate may be cleared.
summary_for_embedding: A raised noticeboard signal recording the operator's decision to select 'Push the drawn style further first' for decision://global/visual-fidelity and stating that gated work may resume.
intention_for_embedding: To communicate the resolved visual-fidelity option and unblock dependent work that was gated on the decision.
